src/flit/platform/spellcheck

  • Index

        checkSpelling
      • checkSpelling(text: string; locale: string = "en-US"): seq[MisspelledRange]
        demoSpellChecker

        setSpellChecker
      • setSpellChecker(fn: SpellChecker)

Spell check. Returns ranges of misspelled words in a string so the TextField (or any text-rendering widget) can draw squiggle underlines.

Native APIs:

  • macOS: NSSpellChecker.sharedSpellChecker.checkSpellingOfString
  • Linux: Hunspell via libhunspell
  • Windows: Windows.Globalization.SpellCheckGenerator

Each is a real binding job. This module exposes the public API and a pluggable provider; ships a tiny built-in checker that flags only words containing weird patterns (no real dictionary). Real integrations register via setSpellChecker(fn).

Types

MisspelledRange = object
  start*: int
  length*: int
  suggestions*: seq[string]
Byte offsets into the checked string for a single misspelled word. suggestions are alternatives (best first); may be empty. 
SpellChecker = proc (text: string; locale: string): seq[MisspelledRange] {.
    closure.}

Vars

activeSpellChecker {.threadvar.}: SpellChecker

Procs

proc checkSpelling(text: string; locale: string = "en-US"): seq[MisspelledRange] {.
    ...raises: [Exception], tags: [RootEffect], forbids: [].}
Returns a sequence of misspelled-word ranges in text. Empty seq means "all good." When no spell-check provider is installed, always returns empty. 
proc demoSpellChecker(text: string; locale: string): seq[MisspelledRange] {.
    ...raises: [], tags: [], forbids: [].}
Demo / smoke-test checker. Flags words with 3+ consecutive identical letters (e.g. "aaaa", "loooong"). Not a real spell check; just enough to verify the underline path works. 
proc setSpellChecker(fn: SpellChecker) {....raises: [], tags: [], forbids: [].}
Installs a spell-check provider. Apps that integrate Hunspell or platform spell check call this once at startup.
